featured.name: SymTorch
featured.tagline: PyTorch library that translates deep learning models into human-readable equations.
featured.why_radar: Finally, someone's tackling the black box problem properly. Instead of just explaining what your model does, SymTorch shows you the actual maths it learned. This could be the bridge between deep learning's power and symbolic AI's interpretability.

featured.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite
featured.tagline: Google's most cost-efficient model with adjustable thinking levels for high-scale production.
featured.why_radar: Google's doubling down on the efficiency game with adjustable reasoning levels. Smart move when everyone's burning cash on inference costs. The tripled pricing compared to the predecessor suggests they've learned something about sustainable AI economics.

picks.name: GPT-5.3 Instant
picks.tagline: OpenAI's model designed for smoother conversations and reduced hallucinations.
picks.why_radar: OpenAI's trying to fix the 'please calm down' problem that's been annoying users for months. Less cringe, better search integration, more natural responses. Incremental but necessary improvements to keep ChatGPT competitive.

picks.name: Claude Code Voice Mode
picks.tagline: Voice capability for Anthropic's AI coding assistant.
picks.why_radar: Anthropic's catching up to the voice trend in coding tools. Makes sense when you're debugging or pair programming to just talk through the problems. Could be particularly useful for code reviews and explaining complex logic.

hn_top5.title: You need to rewrite your CLI for AI agents
hn_top5.summary: CLIs need fundamental redesigns to work properly with AI agents rather than humans. Current command-line interfaces assume interactive human users and fail when automated systems try to use them. This represents a genuine shift in how we need to think about software interfaces.

hn_top5.title: My AI Agents Lie About Their Status, So I Built a Hidden Monitor
hn_top5.summary: AI agents apparently can't be trusted to report their own status accurately, forcing developers to build separate monitoring systems. This highlights a fundamental reliability problem with current AI agent implementations. The need for hidden monitoring suggests these systems are still quite immature.

hn_top5.title: Ask HN: What is the "Control Plane" for local AI agents?
hn_top5.summary: Someone is asking about management systems for coordinating multiple local AI agents. This reflects the growing complexity of AI agent deployments where multiple agents need orchestration. The concept of a 'control plane' suggests people are borrowing ideas from Kubernetes and distributed systems.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: AgentBus – Centralized AI Agent-to-Agent Messaging via REST API
hn_top5.summary: A new service that lets AI agents communicate with each other through REST APIs rather than direct integration. This addresses the real problem of agent coordination but REST APIs seem like overkill for local agent communication. The centralised approach might create bottlenecks.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Term-CLI – interactive terminals for AI agents (for SSH/TUI/REPL flows)
hn_top5.summary: A tool that provides interactive terminal sessions specifically designed for AI agents to use SSH and command-line programs. This tackles the practical problem of agents needing to interact with existing terminal-based tools. It's a sensible bridge between current tooling and AI automation needs.
